type._uniformNote: GPU uniform renamed `type`->`noiseType` because `type` is a reserved keyword in WGSL/HLSL.

colorMode._uniformNote: Declared uniform; the canonical WGSL precompute body IGNORES colorMode (always writes rgb=dist,trap,iter). Carried for parity; the GLSL path branches on it but we port from WGSL.

passes.name: precompute
passes._mrt: drawBuffers:2 — attachment order: color=SV_Target0->volumeCache (vol atlas), geoOut=SV_Target1->geoBuffer (geo: xyz=normal, w=dist).

volumeCache._role: VOLUME (vol) surface: 2D ATLAS holding a 3D voxel field. Atlas = volumeSize wide x volumeSize^2 tall = volumeSize slices of volumeSize x volumeSize (canonical vol0..7 = 64x4096; reference 04 §8). Layout: voxel (x,y,z) -> atlas pixel (x, z*volumeSize + y). Channels: r=normalizedDist, g=orbitTrap, b=iterRatio, a=1 (WGSL ignores colorMode). Max safe volumeSize=128 (height 16384 = D3D11 limit). Downstream render3d/renderLit3d raymarches it.

geoBuffer._role: GEO surface: matching atlas. xyz = normal*0.5+0.5 (analytic SDF gradient, WGSL normalize(-gradient+0.000001)), w = normalizedDist (depth/sdf). Consumed by render/renderLit3d for lighting.

volume._comment: 3D / RENDER tier VOLUME GENERATOR. Single volume-write pass (precompute) renders the Mandelbulb/Mandelcube/Julia voxel field into a 2D atlas (the vol surface) plus an analytic-normal geo atlas, via MRT (drawBuffers:2). The viewport is the atlas size (volumeSize x volumeSize^2), NOT the display resolution. No raymarch, no feedback, no MRT-ping-pong here — this just FILLS the volume; downstream render3d/renderLit3d raymarches the atlas into a 2D image. inheritsVolumeSize: when chained (e.g. noise3d().fractal3d()), the unscoped volumeSize is inherited from upstream.
volume.atlasMapping: voxel(x,y,z) -> atlasPixel(x, z*volumeSize + y); slice = py / volumeSize, row = py % volumeSize

feedback._comment: Single-pass VOLUME-WRITE generator. No feedback, no repeat, no persistent state. Both outputs (volumeCache vol-atlas + geoBuffer geo-atlas) are transient pooled textures (NOT 'global_'), re-rendered whenever a uniform changes. MRT (drawBuffers:2). Atlas height = volumeSize^2 (reference 10 §6.11).
